summary: Frontend-only testing of Dahlia Milano single-page restaurant site. Executed 58 UI assertions covering hero, navbar (incl. glass-on-scroll), 5 pizza items + interactive active image (Spicy Romeo €16 verified), bar section + 4 drinks, 4 events (titles verified), 3 reviews + 6 social tiles, footer, reservation form validation (empty -> error toast, valid -> success toast + form reset), Lenis smooth scroll to #events, horizontal-scroll guard, and mobile menu (390px) including toggle + mobile reserve button. ALL 58 assertions passed.

issues: Mobile dropdown menu has no solid background until navbar adds 'glass' class on scroll >24px. When opened at top of page the nav links visually overlap the hero image, hurting readability. Recommend giving the open mobile drawer its own bg-dahlia-bg/95 backdrop-blur regardless of scroll state.

action_items: Hydration warning in console: <span> rendered as child of <option> in ReservationSection select (Time/Guests). This is caused by the dev-tooling x-* attribute injector wrapping option text in a <span>, which is invalid HTML. If this is from emergent's source-mapping dev tool it can be ignored; otherwise wrap option text differently (e.g., pass plain string children).
Add solid background to the open mobile navbar dropdown so it does not bleed into hero content when opened at the top of the page.
critical_code_review_comments: ReservationSection.jsx: form-reset path is correct but no field-level inline error states are shown — only toast. Adequate for an MVP showcase, but a11y could improve by setting aria-invalid on missing required fields.
Navbar.jsx: mobile drawer relies on parent header's bg which is transparent until scroll. Add explicit background to mobile panel.
App.js uses '@/...' aliases — confirm CRACO/webpack alias is configured (it loads fine, so resolved).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Single Playwright sweep covered every data-testid in the review request. Hero, Navbar (desktop + glass-on-scroll), PizzaShowcase active-state (item-2 -> SPICY ROMEO €16 verified), BarSection, EventsSection (4 expected titles all present and matching), SocialProof (3 reviews + 6 social tiles), Footer, ReservationSection (empty submit error toast + valid submit success toast + form reset all confirmed), Lenis smooth scroll to events, no horizontal overflow, and 390px mobile menu open/visible. Only console noise is a React 'span cannot be child of option' hydration warning generated by emergent dev-tool x-* injector — not a functional bug.
rca of the issue: No functional bugs found. Hydration warning RCA: dev tooling instrumentation wraps <option>'s text children in <span style='display:contents'>; browsers reject <span> inside <option>. Mitigation: ignore in dev (it does not affect production builds) or refactor option to render text via attribute instead of children.
